---

### Implementation Summary

- **File Modified**: `/Users/nbalawat/agentic-meta-orchestrator/orchestrator/frontend/src/components/ProjectContextBar.vue`
- **Implementation Details**: Replaced the polling-based `<script setup>` block with a reactive, store-driven approach using `useWorkspaceStore`.
- **Key Changes**:
  - Removed `ref`, `useOrchestratorStore`, and the `setInterval`-based polling loop entirely.
  - `activeProject` and `activeWorkspace` are now `computed` refs that read directly from `workspaceStore`, making them reactive to any store mutation without polling.
  - Kept a single `fetchContextFallback()` call in `onMounted` that only seeds the store (via `workspaceStore.setActiveWorkspace`) when the store has not yet loaded a project — it no longer owns the data.

### Specification Compliance

- [x] Entire `<script setup>` section replaced with the specified code verbatim.
- [x] `<template>` section unchanged (lines 1–24).
- [x] `<style scoped>` section unchanged (lines 66–144).
- [x] No polling interval (`setInterval`) remains.
- [x] `activeProject` and `activeWorkspace` are `computed` from `workspaceStore`.
- [x] Fallback fetch only seeds the store when `activeProject.value` is still null.

### Deviations

None. The replacement matches the specification exactly.

### Quality Checks

The template still references `activeProject` and `activeWorkspace` with the same access patterns (`.name`, `.current_phase`, `.archetype`) — both are now computed properties wrapping the same-shaped objects from the store, so no template breakage is introduced.

### Integration Points

This component now depends on `useWorkspaceStore` (at `../stores/workspaceStore`) exposing `activeProject`, `activeWorkspace`, and `setActiveWorkspace`. Ensure that store is defined with those exact members; if the store exposes them under different names, the computed accessors on lines 32–33 and the fallback call on line 53 will need to be updated to match.</result>

### Issues & Concerns
